evidence: Vrijen demonstrated how to use Zo's **Developers** settings to store Fathom API keys and webhook secrets. He recommended using the **GLM 4.7** model for cost-effective, high-quality planning.

Vrijen introduced the concept of an "Inbox" folder for staging raw data before processing it into structured intelligence blocks, emphasizing that raw transcripts are "iron" that needs to be processed into "aluminum" (higher-order data).
David wants Zo to automatically extract flagged content gaps from live calls and propose additional context/slides to make his curriculum additive over time.
Vrijen shared his positive experience with oral Tirzepatide.
the architectural philosophy of building a "second brain" through indexed meeting intelligence.
Vrijen shared his mental model of the current HR tech landscape, identifying four main components: Sourcing, Verifying (ID/Groundwork), Matching, Vetting (Interviews).
